retry_reason: kref_put always succeeds; failure is not possible.
contract_summary: This is a put wrapper that releases a reference on an amdgpu_reset_domain object. It is safe to call with a NULL pointer (no-op). When the last reference is released, the object is destroyed via the registered release function. After calling this function, the caller must no longer use the domain pointer unless they hold another reference. This function does not return a value, so the caller cannot determine if the object was freed.

judge.reason: The sole purpose is to decrement the reference count on an amdgpu_reset_domain via kref_put, with a NULL check. No other logic is present.

check.reason: The kref_put is called with &domain->refcount, which decrements the refcount inside domain. The access path domain.refcount.refcount and type chain amdgpu_reset_domain--kref--refcount_t correctly trace from the parameter domain to the refcount_t field. The operation is a put.
